text:
Portuguese triathlete Vasco Vilaca says he has been suffering with a ‘pretty bad stomach flu’ after swimming in the River Seine at theParisOlympics.

Alex Yee delivered one of the most memorable moments of the Games from a Team GB perspectiveas he staged a stunning late fightback to win a compelling men’s triathlon in the French capital last week.

However, there were serious doubts that the men and women’s triathlons could even go ahead in the build-up, with practice sessions having to be scrapped as a result of dangerously high levels of E. Coli in the water.

And while Olympicorganisers shelled out a total of £1.2 billionin a desperate attempt to clean up the river for the triathlons’ scheduled start times, the men’s event had to be postponed for 24 hours to ensure the safety of the competing athletes.

Shortly after Yee secured Team GB’s fifth gold of the Games,Tyler Mislawchuk of Canada was pictured throwing upat the finishing line, raising further concerns about the Seine.

New Zealand’s Hayden Wilde – the silver medallist – and Ainsley Thorpe have since been struggling with bacterial infections and the latter almost had to pull out of Monday’s mixed relay as a result of his sickness.

This afternoon, Portugal’s Vilaca revealed that he was the latest casualty off the back of last Wednesday’s triathlon.

‘Unfortunately since Tuesday evening I’ve developed symptoms consistent with a pretty bad stomach flu,’ Vilaca told his followers on Instagram.

‘Vomiting, diarrhoea, headache and stomach aches is not the way I wanted to leave Paris.’

Despite his illness, Vilaca is ‘happy and proud’ to have been given the opportunity represent Portugal on the global stage.

‘Luckily I am getting all the help I need from my NOC doctors and staff for a fast recovery.

‘The symptoms will go away but the 3 Olympic diplomas earned but Team Portugal will live forever!

‘Leaving Paris happy and proud to be part of this team.’

title: Another triathlete falls ill after swimming in River Seine at Olympics | Metro News
description: Portuguese triathlete Vasco Vilaca says he has been suffering with a 'pretty bad stomach flu' after swimming in the Seine at the Olympics.
